All right, the three pillar pitch. So those of you who have been with us for a while, this will be familiar to you and for those of you who are new, this is the three pillars of everything that you sell. So fitness, nutrition, and accountability are the three main pillars of any fitness program. The only difference in how we're selling it for a hybrid versus how we're selling it in person is just the fitness component is going to be a little bit different. Everything else is the same. And so once we've exhausted the pain cycle, we've gone through, okay, what have you done? How did that work for you? What have you done? How did that work for you? How long did that work? Blah, blah, blah, blah. You go through all those things. You should, with each of the things that they're naming, be thinking in your mind, fitness, nutrition, and accountability. And you should be checking off what they are. And so after you've mined everything, then you're like, okay, cool. So let me tell you a little bit about the program that we have. Is that okay? Okay, cool. So always let them, like ask if it's okay to tell them about the program. Fantastic. All right. So fitness-wise, so you did Jenny Craig, right? So now you go, after asking, you now assess what they've done, right? So you're assessing, okay, so Jenny Craig, you had nutrition and you had accountability, but you didn't have the fitness component, right? And so that was the piece that was missing, and that's why you couldn't stick with it because you lost muscle and then you couldn't gain it back, and then you gained more weight, right? Right. Okay. So then in another time, you joined a gym membership, but you couldn't stick with the workouts because no one was holding you accountable and you didn't have anything on your food, right? Right. So same thing there. And then the other time you tried your shakes program, and there you didn't get really any workouts, no one was really following up with you, and like, I mean, really, you had almost zero out of three, like half out of three on that one, right? Ha, ha, ha, right. Cool. Got it. Okay. So I think you're going to be really good fit for this program. Can I tell you more about it, like how arch works and why, like, we have such a high success rate why most people hit the goal in six weeks or less? Yeah? Okay, cool. Fantastic. All right. So right now you're, you know, you're 200 pounds, and so at the end of the six weeks, I would expect you to be at 175 or lower. That's okay? You want to be upset with me? Okay, cool, because that's very common here. So I just want to make sure that you're not upset. Okay, cool. So three main pieces, right? I've already covered fitness, nutrition, accountability. So fitness-wise, you just got to commit to being open to exercising and allow me to find out what you like. All right? So the difference between this and like a group workout type thing, right? Like if you go there, you just got to do whatever they're doing that day, right? You're going to go and get beat up, and that's what it's going to be, right? And if you're working out of 5 a.m., you got to keep going out of 5 a.m., right? The problem with that is that just like you've already seen in the past, it's not sustainable, right? So the key for us is to find exercise lifestyle fit. And the way that I like to think about this is Netflix, right? So bear with me. So do you have any shows that you watch? Is there anything that you like, you know, any shows that you absolutely love? Okay, you like Hossa Cards. Okay, fantastic. So are you ever, do you ever think to yourself like, oh my God, I'm just really trying to get motivated to watch Hossa Cards. I just can't get the motivation to watch it, right? Of course not, right? Because you look forward to watching it, right? And when you look forward to things, you always make time to do them, right? But the thing is, if your fitness is like that and you actually look forward to it, then you always stick with it, right? We always do the things that we like doing. And so the key is to figure out which one you like. And so let me give you a different example. If you've, let's say you had weird, you know, red bean flavored ice cream, right? The first time you had ice cream, you might think to yourself, man, ice cream sucks when in fact you just have the wrong flavor, right? Ice cream is actually awesome, right? And so I like to think about exercises in ways like you maybe try a couple things, but you haven't really found the right flavor. And so it's my job as your coach to figure out what you like so that you can actually stick with it, so that it feels like Netflix, so that you feel like doing it. And that's the goal. And the thing is, is that can even change over time, right? Because maybe, maybe in the beginning, it's like, let's just walk 10 minutes a day. You like that you get outside, you get some fresh air, you're like, cool, I can do this, I can commit to this. Because in the day, if you only work out for six weeks or eight weeks, so what? Right? In an 80 year lifespan, right? And you only work out two times for six months straight, who cares, right? So the point for me is to experiment with you and coach you through it so that you can never fall off. Or you can never fall off because if you don't like something, we just find something else, right? And we keep going until we find what you really like. And I promise you, everyone likes exercising. We just have to figure out what thing is right for you. Does that make sense? Okay. So you've got to be willing to work out. Okay, great. Second one is nutrition. Nutrition-wise, I'm going to make you a personal plan that's sustainable. All right? So all the things you did in the past were all things that were short periods of time. You lost a lot of weight or you didn't lose weight because you can stick with it, right? Because you wouldn't walk in the door if you would have sustained it, right? If you kept eating like that forever, you would be, you'd still be good, right? Of course. Okay. So sustainable nutrition is about trying to find the few things that we can change in your diet that will make the biggest results. So that the fewer things we change, the more likely you are to stick with it and succeed in the long run. And slowly trade things in and out so that we can get you to where you want to go. Does that make sense? Yes? Okay. So I'm not saying we need to re-haul everything. You just need to change just your breakfast. Just that, right? And that's it. And just stay consistent with that for a week or two, right? And we'll see how it goes. And we'll measure and adjust. And that's why communication with me is so important. All right? Does that make sense though? So fitness-wise, finding something, finding the flavor of fitness that you like so you actually look forward to it so it feels like Netflix. You're good with that? Okay, cool. Extra, as for nutrition-wise, is actually making sure that you can eat the foods you like. Because if you can never eat cake for the rest of your life, you know that you're screwed. Right? Like, I mean, if I can't eat cookie dough for the rest of my life, I'm gonna give up. I'm gonna say, you know what? I'm gonna be overweight. It is what it is. Right? Right. So we're just gonna find out how we can fit the things that you like and then maybe trade some of the other meals that you're not as interested in so that you can eat at your daughter's 16th birthday or you can have a slice of cake at your graduation. Because what's the point of living if you can't live in the moments that matter? Right? Cool. So then the third one, which kind of leads me to what we're talking about, is like, I'll talk about communication, is accountability. Right? And so up to this point, it's sticking with it. Right? That's been the issue. So first of all, it's the design, which we went from the fitness and nutrition part, but also just the coaching to make adjustments. Right? And so the goal for us with accountability is to take it from external accountability, which is me and money, right? To internal motivation. External accountability, me and money, to internal motivation. All right? So we're going to try and jumpstart that motor, and this is me tugging. It's your money and me who are trying to pull in the lawnmower to get that internal motor going. But once it gets going, then you can go on your own. And so my goal is that at the end of this thing, you won't need me anymore. Right? And it might take six months. It might take 12 months. But by the end of this, my goal is that you're like, you know what, Alex? You're the man. I love you. I'm fit for life. Right? I get what I need to do because this isn't hard for me anymore because I understand it. Right? So the way that we do that is really communication. All right? So what I need you to commit to me for is that you're going to weigh in every morning. All right? And you're going to text me a picture of your scale weight. Cool? Can you commit to that? Okay. Fantastic. And that also makes sure that if you always have regular fluctuations, we can account for that. And that way we can take averages so we can see over longer periods of time where the trends are. Does that make sense? Okay. Great. The next one is that you're going to come in here into the facility with me twice a month. Okay? So every other week, you're going to come in. We'll just have that as a standing appointment that you come in with me. I'll take your measurements. We'll take some progress pictures just to make sure we're making progress. And so that you can look six months from now and I can show you these pictures because no one sees it on a week to week basis. When I show you six months ago, you're going to be like, oh my gosh. So six months from now, we'll look back at today and you're like, oh my gosh, I can't believe that's what it looks like. Oh my god, but it's okay because we're going to get there. All right? Cool. So those are really the primary things. And then weekly, I just asked for a stats report, which is just a little bit more like, hey, how are you sleeping? How's your energy? You're stressed. How are you doing with food? How are you getting cravings? Things like that. And that just helps me gauge where you're at so we can make adjustments. Does that make sense? So fitness-wise, we're going to find exactly what flavor you like so it feels like Netflix. Nutrition-wise, we're going to figure out a way so that you can have your cake and eat it too. All right? And then accountability-wise, I'm going to be in constant communication with you so we can make adjustments so that we get you to where you want to go. Okay? And that we can make those so we can eventually get it so that you don't even need me. All right? That's the goal. In the beginning, it's going to be more work, right? But over time, it's going to be less and less because you're going to be more and more self-guided. All right? Fantastic. All right. So that is the three-pillar pitch. That's how the program works. And then we go to the close.
